en.title: Spoken script
en.prompt: write an article about [TOPIC] in a human-like style, simple English, using contractions, idioms, transitional phrases, interjections, dangling modifiers, and colloquialisms and avoiding repetitive phrases and unnatural sentence structures.
en.remark: write scripts for live broadcasts, videos, podcasts and other types of spoken content. Contributed by @Bettycroco.

metaDescription: Writing scripts? Create human-like articles with idioms and colloquialisms. Avoid unnatural repetition for spoken content.

faq.q: Does the voiceover script read smoothly?
faq.a: Relatively smooth (the prompt requires conversational tone, interjections, connectives), but text generation still lacks true spoken pacing. After reading aloud and recording, break apart awkward spots and add pauses. Viral self-media scripts are always iterated this way.

faq.q: Can I specify duration or word count?
faq.a: Append "1-minute voiceover, Chinese around 240 characters including punctuation." Without word count the AI expands freely. Overrun on short videos or underrun on long podcasts are both problems. Always state the duration target.
